title: LSP features — hover, go-to-def, completions
analysis:
Task 32 built the LSP server foundation: ReifyLanguageServer with full-sync document management, diagnostic pipeline (parse→compile→check→convert), and bidirectional position/span conversion utilities including position_to_offset() and find_word_at_offset() (added as pre-1 for this task, already committed as 70772d8).

This task adds three interactive LSP features: hover, go-to-definition, and completions.

**Architecture:** All three features need the same underlying data: ParsedModule (for declaration names and source spans), CompiledModule (for resolved types via ValueCellDecl.cell_type), and optionally CheckResult (for evaluated runtime values). Rather than duplicating the pipeline in each module, a shared AnalysisContext struct runs parse→compile→check once and provides structured accessors. This context also extracts the module_name_from_uri helper currently inlined in diagnostics.rs.

**Key constraint — compiler discards spans:** The compiler sets ALL spans to SourceSpan::new(0,0) in ValueCellDecl, CompiledConstraint, and RealizationDecl. Go-to-definition MUST use ParsedModule spans (ParamDecl.span, LetDecl.span) which retain accurate byte offsets from tree-sitter. For type info, CompiledModule.templates[].value_cells[] provides cell_type: Type.

**Symbol lookup strategy:** Word-based extraction via find_word_at_offset, then name matching against known symbols. This is pragmatic because: (a) ParsedModule declaration spans cover entire declarations, not individual name tokens; (b) it handles both declaration-site and reference-site hovers uniformly; (c) no need for full AST position walking.

**Value formatting:** Value doesn't implement Display. A local format_value() helper in the analysis module handles user-friendly formatting (Scalar{si_value: 0.08, dimension: LENGTH} → '0.08 m').

**Test strategy:** All tests use bracket_source() fixture with known byte offsets from bracket_parsed_module(): width param at (24,42), height at (47,74), thickness at (79,107), volume let at (187,226), 'width' ident ref at (200,205), 'thickness' ref in constraint at (243,252). Tests verify exact hover content, goto-def target locations, and completion item counts/kinds.

prerequisites.description: Add position_to_offset (LSP Position → byte offset) and find_word_at_offset (extract identifier word at byte offset) utilities to convert.rs. Foundational for all three features.
tests: position_to_offset first line returns correct byte offset
position_to_offset second line accounts for newline
position_to_offset at origin returns 0
position_to_offset past end returns source.len()
position_to_offset handles UTF-16 multibyte (emoji)
find_word_at_offset returns full word from middle position
find_word_at_offset at non-ident returns None
find_word_at_offset at start/end of word returns full word
find_word_at_offset on keyword 'param' returns keyword string

prerequisites.description: Create analysis.rs module with shared AnalysisContext that runs parse→compile→check pipeline once and provides structured accessors. Includes: module_name_from_uri(uri) helper extracted from diagnostics.rs; AnalysisContext struct holding source ref, uri ref, ParsedModule, CompiledModule, CheckResult; methods: new(source, uri) -> Self, find_member_decl(name) -> Option<MemberInfo> (returns kind, type, span from ParsedModule+CompiledModule), structure_names() -> Vec<(&str, member counts)>, member_names() -> Vec<(&str, ValueCellKind, &Type)>, get_value(entity, member) -> Option<&Value>; format_value(value: &Value) -> String helper. Update diagnostics.rs to use module_name_from_uri. Register module in lib.rs.
tests: analysis context builds without panic on bracket_source
analysis context builds without panic on empty source
find_member_decl('width') returns Param kind, Scalar[Length] type, span (24,42)
find_member_decl('volume') returns Let kind with inferred type
find_member_decl('nonexistent') returns None
member_names returns all 6 value cells (5 params + volume)
structure_names returns [('Bracket', 5 params, 1 let, 3 constraints)]
get_value returns evaluated Value for 'width' (Scalar 0.08m)
format_value formats Scalar LENGTH as SI meters
format_value formats Bool/Int/Real/String/Undef correctly
module_name_from_uri extracts 'test' from 'file:///test.ri'

steps.description: Write failing tests for hover on param declaration: (1) hover at Position corresponding to byte ~30 ('width' in 'param width: Scalar = 80mm') returns Some(Hover) with markdown containing 'param', 'width', 'Scalar[Length]'; (2) hover at Position for 'thickness' returns similar info; (3) hover at whitespace/operator position returns None.

Create hover.rs with compute_hover(source: &str, uri: &Url, position: Position) -> Option<Hover>. Uses position_to_offset → find_word_at_offset → build AnalysisContext → look up word via find_member_decl → format markdown with kind, name, type, and evaluated value. Returns Hover with MarkupContent::markdown and range covering the matched word. Register module in lib.rs.

Write failing tests for hover on let binding and ident references: (1) hover on 'volume' (at byte ~193) shows 'let', 'volume', computed type; (2) hover on 'thickness' at byte 243 (inside constraint expression 'constraint thickness > 2mm') shows param type info identical to hovering at declaration site; (3) hover on 'width' at byte 200 (inside 'let volume = width * ...') shows param info.

Extend hover.rs to include evaluated values from CheckResult in the hover output. For lets, show the computed value. For params, show the default value. Verify that word-based lookup correctly handles ident references (same member name match whether at declaration or reference site).

Write failing tests for hover edge cases: (1) hover on structure name 'Bracket' (byte ~10) shows structure summary with member counts; (2) hover on keyword 'param' (byte 24) shows keyword description; (3) hover on keyword 'constraint' shows keyword description; (4) hover on unknown/non-identifier word returns None; (5) hover on empty source returns None.

Extend hover.rs: after member lookup, check if word matches a structure name → show 'structure Bracket (5 params, 1 let, 3 constraints)'. Then check keyword list → show brief description. Keywords: structure, param, let, constraint, sub, import, if, then, else, and, or, not, true, false, auto. Return None for unrecognized words.

Write failing tests for go-to-definition: (1) goto-def on 'thickness' at byte 243 (in 'constraint thickness > 2mm') returns Location with range corresponding to span (79,107); (2) goto-def on 'width' at byte 286 (in 'width / 4') returns Location for span (24,42); (3) goto-def on 'volume' reference returns Location for span (187,226); (4) goto-def on keyword 'param' returns None; (5) goto-def on structure name 'Bracket' returns None; (6) goto-def on unknown word returns None.

Create goto_def.rs with compute_goto_definition(source: &str, uri: &Url, position: Position) -> Option<Location>. Uses find_word_at_offset → parse source → iterate ParsedModule declarations → for each StructureDef search members for ParamDecl/LetDecl with matching name → return Location(uri, span_to_range(decl.span)). Only needs ParsedModule (no compile/check needed). Register module in lib.rs.

Write failing tests for completions: (1) completions include all 12+ keywords with CompletionItemKind::KEYWORD; (2) completions include 6 scope identifiers (width, height, thickness, fillet_radius, hole_diameter, volume) with VARIABLE kind and type detail string; (3) completions include 'Bracket' with STRUCT kind; (4) completions include built-in functions (box, cylinder, sphere, sin, cos, sqrt, abs, min, max) with FUNCTION kind; (5) completions include type names (Scalar, Bool, Int, Real, String) with CLASS kind; (6) completions on empty source still includes keywords and built-ins.

Create completion.rs with compute_completions(source: &str, uri: &Url, position: Position) -> Vec<CompletionItem>. Builds AnalysisContext, collects: (a) keywords as KEYWORD items, (b) value_cell members as VARIABLE items with cell_type.to_string() as detail, (c) structure names as STRUCT items, (d) built-in function names as FUNCTION items, (e) type names as CLASS items. Returns flat list for client-side filtering. Register module in lib.rs.

Write failing test: after server.initialize(), capabilities should include hover_provider (Some), definition_provider (Some), and completion_provider (Some). Uses existing LspService::new(ReifyLanguageServer::new) + service.inner() pattern.

Update server.rs initialize() to advertise: hover_provider: Some(HoverProviderCapability::Simple(true)), definition_provider: Some(OneOf::Left(true)), completion_provider: Some(CompletionOptions::default()). Existing text_document_sync unchanged.

Write failing integration tests: (1) open bracket_source, call hover() with position at 'width' → returns Ok(Some(Hover)) with non-empty contents; (2) open bracket_source, call goto_definition() at 'thickness' in constraint → returns Ok(Some(GotoDefinitionResponse)); (3) open bracket_source, call completion() → returns Ok(Some(CompletionResponse)) with items. These tests verify end-to-end wiring through the server handlers.

Wire handlers in server.rs: (1) async fn hover(&self, params: HoverParams) → reads document from state, calls hover::compute_hover(text, uri, position), returns result; (2) async fn goto_definition(&self, params: GotoDefinitionParams) → calls goto_def::compute_goto_definition, wraps in GotoDefinitionResponse::Scalar; (3) async fn completion(&self, params: CompletionParams) → calls completion::compute_completions, wraps in CompletionResponse::Array. Each follows existing did_open pattern of state.read().await → documents.get().

design_decisions.decision: Shared AnalysisContext runs the pipeline once per request
design_decisions.rationale: Hover, goto-def (optionally), and completions all need ParsedModule, CompiledModule, and/or CheckResult. A shared context avoids duplicating the 5-line pipeline (derive module name, parse, compile, check). This also centralizes the module_name_from_uri extraction, reducing duplication with diagnostics.rs. The context is cheap to construct per-request given M1's single-file scope.

design_decisions.decision: Word-based symbol lookup rather than full AST position walking
design_decisions.rationale: ParsedModule stores whole-declaration spans (e.g., ParamDecl.span covers entire 'param width: Scalar = 80mm', not just 'width'). Walking the AST to find which node contains the cursor offset would miss declaration-site name hovers and require complex tree traversal. Word extraction + name matching is simpler, covers all cases (declaration site, reference site, structure names, keywords), and handles the fact that expression-level ExprKind::Ident spans are only available deep in the AST.

The compiler sets ALL spans to SourceSpan::new(0,0) in compile_structure() (lines 669, 683, 705, 729, 751 in lib.rs). ParsedModule retains accurate tree-sitter byte offsets: ParamDecl.span for 'param width' is (24,42), LetDecl.span for 'let volume' is (187,226), etc. This is the only reliable source of declaration locations. Go-to-def only needs the parsed module, not the compiled one.

design_decisions.decision: Hover shows evaluated values from CheckResult
design_decisions.rationale: The task description says 'type/value info'. Running the full pipeline through Engine::check() provides both Type (from CompiledModule) and evaluated Value (from CheckResult.values). For a CAD language, showing computed dimensions immediately on hover is high-value: 'param width: Scalar[Length] = 0.08 m'. The cost of running check() is negligible for M1 single-file sources.

design_decisions.decision: Value formatting as a local helper, not cross-crate Display impl
design_decisions.rationale: Value is defined in reify-types (outside module scope). Adding Display there would be a cross-crate change. A local format_value() in analysis.rs handles the LSP-specific formatting needs: Scalar shows SI value with dimension label, Bool shows true/false, Undef shows '(undefined)'. This can be promoted to a proper Display impl later if useful elsewhere.

design_decisions.decision: Flat completion list with client-side filtering
design_decisions.rationale: Always return all completions (keywords, identifiers, types, functions) regardless of cursor position. LSP clients perform their own fuzzy filtering on CompletionItem.label. Server-side context-aware filtering (only types after ':', only keywords at start of member) adds complexity with minimal benefit since clients already filter well. Can be added as a refinement later.

design_decisions.decision: Go-to-def returns None for structure names and keywords
design_decisions.rationale: Structure names are self-referential (Bracket is its own definition). Keywords have no source definition. Only param/let member names provide meaningful navigation targets. This matches user expectations: clicking an identifier reference navigates to its declaration.

design_decisions.decision: Fresh Engine per request, consistent with diagnostics pattern
design_decisions.rationale: Engine is not Send (holds Box<dyn ConstraintChecker>). Persisting across async handler calls would require a dedicated thread, Mutex, or architectural change. For M1 single-file sources the per-request cost is negligible. M4 'Living Design' task will address persistent Engine state for warm-starting and incremental evaluation.

reuse.how: Convert incoming LSP cursor position to byte offset for word extraction. First step in all three feature handlers. Added in pre-1 (commit 70772d8).
Extract identifier word at cursor position. Used by hover and goto-def to find the symbol name under cursor. Added in pre-1 (commit 70772d8).
Convert ParsedModule declaration spans to LSP Range for goto-def Location results and hover word Range.

The module_name_from_uri derivation (uri.path_segments()...strip_suffix('.ri')) and the parse→compile→check sequence are extracted into AnalysisContext. diagnostics.rs is updated to use the shared helper.
Type implements Display: Bool→'Bool', Scalar{LENGTH}→'Scalar[Length]', etc. Used in hover markdown and completion item detail strings via .to_string().

Primary test fixture with known byte offsets: width param (24,42), height (47,74), thickness (79,107), fillet_radius (112,144), hole_diameter (149,181), volume let (187,226), body let (346,385), 'width' ident in volume expr (200,205), 'thickness' ident in constraint (243,252).

Integration tests use LspService::new(ReifyLanguageServer::new), service.inner() for direct handler calls, did_open to load documents. Existing pattern reused for hover/goto_def/completion handler tests.

TopologyTemplate.value_cells provides cell_type: Type and kind: ValueCellKind for each declared member. ValueCellDecl.id.member gives the name. Used by AnalysisContext to build symbol→type mapping for hover and completions.
CheckResult.values is a ValueMap (PersistentMap<ValueCellId, Value>) providing evaluated runtime values. Used by hover to show computed values alongside types.
